phases.name: Safe Modification Zones
phases.description: Explicit AI mutation boundaries with machine-checkable rules

CORE.rationale: Core framework changes require framework updates

APP.description: Application layer - safe modifications within contracts

PROVIDER.description: Service providers - can be overridden via container

constraints: Cannot override other plugins without explicit rule

api_surface.description: Public API endpoints that app can depend on

swx_zone_info.description: Show zone classification for a file path

swx_zone_validate.description: Validate zone integrity and detect unauthorized modifications

swx_zone_check.description: Check if operation is allowed on path

phases.name: File Role Metadata
phases.description: Structured headers and sidecar metadata for AI context

SWX_ROLE.description: Primary role classification of the file

example_headers.service_example:
# SWX_ROLE: Service
# SWX_SCOPE: Application
# SWX_DEPENDS_ON: [UserRepository, CacheInterface, Config]
# SWX_MUTATION_ZONE: APP
# SWX_CONTRACTS: [UserServiceProtocol]
# SWX_EXPORTS: [UserService, UserNotFoundError]
# SWX_EVENTS: [{name: user.created, payload: {user_id: int}}]
example_headers.guard_example:
# SWX_ROLE: Guard
# SWX_SCOPE: Request
# SWX_DEPENDS_ON: [TokenService, Config, UserRepository]
# SWX_MUTATION_ZONE: CORE
# SWX_EXPORTS: [JWTGuard, TokenExpiredError, InvalidTokenError]
example_headers.repository_example:
# SWX_ROLE: Repository
# SWX_SCOPE: Infrastructure
# SWX_DEPENDS_ON: [Database, Model]
# SWX_MUTATION_ZONE: APP
# SWX_CONTRACTS: [UserRepositoryProtocol]
# SWX_EXPORTS: [UserRepository]

swx_override_of.description: If this file overrides a core component

swx_metadata_export.description: Export all metadata as machine-readable format

phases.name: Contract Registry
phases.description: Machine-readable interface definitions for AI code generation

contracts.name: RepositoryProtocol
contracts.description: Base repository interface for all data access

methods.name: find_by_id
methods.override_instructions: Must query database by primary key. Return None if not found. Do not raise exceptions for missing records.

methods.name: find_all
methods.signature: find_all(self, filters: Optional[dict] = None, pagination: Optional[Pagination] = None) -> List[Model]
methods.override_instructions: Apply filters and pagination. Return empty list if no results.

methods.name: create
methods.override_instructions: Create new record from data dict. Return created instance with generated ID.

methods.name: update
methods.signature: update(self, id: int, data: dict) -> Optional[Model]
methods.override_instructions: Update existing record. Return updated instance or None if not found.

methods.name: delete
methods.override_instructions: Delete record by ID. Return True if deleted, False if not found.

phases.description: Compressed architecture summary optimized for LLM token limits

examples: swx plan-change "Add multi-tenant rate limit override"
swx plan-change "Add new payment provider" --format json

files_to_modify.description: Create custom rate limiter for multi-tenancy

factors: No breaking changes to existing API

phases.description: Service, event, and plugin dependency graphs in multiple formats

mermaid.example:
graph LR
  rate_limiter --> config
  rate_limiter --> redis
  auth_service --> user_repository

core_immutability.description: Core framework files cannot be modified

plugin_isolation.description: Plugins cannot override other plugins without explicit rule

provider_override.description: Providers can be overridden via container binding only

swx_rules_check.description: Check current codebase for rule violations

phases.name: Agent Instruction Manifest
phases.description: Operating manual for AI agents interacting with SwX

mutation_rules.description: Rules governing what AI agents can modify

rules.rule: Never modify files in CORE zone directly
rules.rationale: Core framework changes require framework updates
rules.exception: Use provider override or plugin extension instead

rules.rule: All APP modifications must implement contract interfaces
Provider modifications must go through container binding

file_ownership_rules.description: Which files agents can modify based on zone

patterns.when_to_use: Need different implementation of core service
Need to react to system events

rules.rule: Method signatures must match contract exactly
Return types must be covariant with contract

testing_requirements.description: Required tests for different change types

example_output.manifest:
# SwX Agent Instruction Manifest

## Mutation Rules

### Rule MUTATION-001: Core Immutability
- **Rule**: Never modify files in CORE zone directly
- **Rationale**: Core framework changes require framework updates
- **Exception**: Use provider override or plugin extension instead

### Rule MUTATION-002: Contract Implementation
- **Rule**: All APP modifications must implement contract interfaces
- **Rationale**: Ensures compatibility and substitutability

## Safe Override Patterns

### Provider Override
```python
container.bind('service', CustomImplementation)
```
Use when: Need different implementation of core service

### Event Listener
```python
class CustomListener(Listener):
    event = 'user.created'
```
Use when: Need to react to system events

## Testing Requirements

| Change Type | Required Tests |
|------------|----------------|
| New Service | unit_test, integration_test |
| New Route | unit_test, api_test |
| Contract Implementation | contract_test |

phases.name: Versioned AI Spec
phases.description: Versioned schema specifications for AI layer compatibility

implementation_priority.rationale: Foundation - all other phases depend on architecture introspection
Safety boundaries - critical for AI modification control
Versioning - ensures compatibility as system evolves
Graphs - enables AI reasoning about dependencies

principles.name: Schema Versioning
principles.description: All exported schemas include version for compatibility checking
principles.implementation: Semver in every export, minimum version requirement in imports

principles.name: Non-Breaking Additions
principles.description: New fields can be added without breaking existing parsers

principles.name: Deprecation Path
principles.implementation: 2-version notice before removal, explicit replacement guidance

principles.name: Multi-Version Support
principles.description: Export system supports multiple schema versions
principles.implementation: Version parameter in CLI, backward-compatible parsing

changes: Renamed plugins to extension_points in agent context
Added zone metadata to all exports
migration_steps: Update AI agents to parse new zone field
Update change planning to respect zone boundaries
